title: Quick MBTI-Style Quiz

introduction.subtitle: A two-minute, MBTI-style snapshot of your four preference dimensions. Original items — not the official MBTI.
introduction.description:
Not the official MBTI assessment. Original items inspired by Jungian type concepts. The result is a simplified four-letter code, useful for conversation and team dynamics — not a clinical read.

Pick the statement that fits you more often, in more contexts. If you are genuinely split, trust your quieter instinct.

Use the result as language for reflection and conversation, not as a permanent label. The terms are meant to make patterns easier to notice, compare, and discuss.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is your choice.
introduction.disclaimer: Inspired by Jungian type theory and MBTI concepts. This is not the official MBTI assessment. Items are original.

questions.text: At a party where you know few people, you tend to...

options.text: Mingle and meet many new people.
Find a few people and have deeper conversations.

conceptHelp.title: Extraversion and introversion: At a party where you know few people, you tend to...
conceptHelp.summary: Extraversion and introversion means where attention and energy tend to go first: outward social contact and breadth, or quieter reflection and depth. This question turns that pair into the everyday situation "At a party where you know few people, you tend to...".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: MBTI-style pairs are useful only as reflection language. They should help you notice a tendency and its trade-off, not label your personality permanently.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Answer by your normal first pull in this situation, not by the version of yourself you think is more mature or impressive.
conceptHelp.example: The options contrast Mingle and meet many new people. versus Find a few people and have deeper conversations.; your answer shows which side of the preference pair you tend to trust first.

questions.text: After a full week of meetings, you recharge by...

options.text: More social time, ideally with good energy.

questions.text: When you start something new, you prefer...

options.text: Step-by-step instructions and concrete examples.

attribution.text: Inspired by Jungian type theory and MBTI concepts. This is not the official MBTI assessment. Items are original.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
